source_jobs.title: GNC Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Valinor is a new type of defense and government tech company focused on solving the problems others don't. Our unique model of centralized go-to-market and decentralized engineering allows us to move with unparalleled efficiency to identify unmet needs, build right-sized solutions, and get products where they matter most - in the hands of users. Unlike others in the space, who focus on the flashy problems, Valinor exists to solve the quiet, unaddressed problems-the small but significant ones that lead to cracks in the country's strength, stability, and security.

Backed by General Catalyst, Founders Fund, and Friends & Family Capital, Valinor was founded in 2024 and has established strategic partnerships with Palantir, Anduril, and Helsing. We are building the dream team, and we want you on it.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Valinor Condor is building the next generation of autonomous VTOL aircraft for defense and enterprise applications. We're a small, technical team moving fast — our vehicle is flying, our customers are real, and the work spans simulation to real life.

We're seeking a Flight Controls & GNC Engineer to own the guidance, navigation, and control stack on our VTOL platform. This is a senior individual contributor role with direct ownership of flight performance — from control law architecture to day-of-demo parameter tuning.

You'll work closely with the Chief Engineer and a small cross-functional team. You'll be our technical authority on everything from transition logic and rate controller design to aero model development and stability analysis.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

Flight Controls & Tuning

 - Own the full GNC stack on our drone: PX4 code, VTOL transition logic, fixed-wing control laws (TECS, NPFG, attitude/rate loops), multicopter controller

 - Design, implement, and iterate on control laws; translate theoretical controller designs into flight-validated parameters, and test them in the field to tune the aircraft

 - Conduct structured flight test campaigns — define test cards, instrument logging, execute in-field, and close the loop with data-driven analysis

 - Debug flight anomalies from logs: identify root cause across sensor, estimator, controller, and actuator layers

Dynamics & Aero Modeling

 - Build and maintain the flight dynamics model; extract and validate aerodynamic coefficients from flight data and CFD outputs

 - Write technically precise scopes of work for CFD contractors; review and integrate their outputs into the aero model

 - Develop and maintain a Simulink-based 6DOF simulation environment for control law prototyping, linearization, and stability margin analysis — de-risking parameter changes before they go to hardware

Systems & Integration

 - Work with the software team on autopilot integration, sensor fusion, and estimator configuration (EKF tuning, IMU/GPS/baro)

 - Support customer demonstrations — ensure the vehicle performs reliably and gracefully under operational conditions




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

Required

 - 4+ years of hands-on flight controls or GNC engineering experience on real flying hardware

 - Deep familiarity with PX4 (or comparable open-source autopilot) — you've tuned it, debugged it, and modified it

 - Experience with fixed-wing or VTOL platforms

 - Ability to read and interpret flight logs to diagnose control system issues (PX4 log analysis, PlotJuggler)

 - Familiarity with aerodynamic modeling pipelines (CFD output → stability derivatives → flight dynamics model)

 - Scripting ability in Python for log analysis, simulation, and tooling; MATLAB/Simulink experience for control design and 6DOF modeling is equally valued

 - Strong fundamentals: classical and modern control theory, rigid body dynamics, state estimation

 - Comfortable in the field, you've executed flight tests, not just designed them on paper

Strongly Preferred

 - Experience with TECS, NPFG, or L1 guidance implementations

 - Exposure to EKF-based state estimators (GPS/IMU/baro fusion)

 - Experienc

source_jobs.title: Fabrication Lead
source_jobs.description:
Valinor is a new type of defense and government tech company focused on solving the problems others don't. Our unique model of centralized go-to-market and decentralized engineering allows us to move with unparalleled efficiency to identify unmet needs, build right-sized solutions, and get products where they matter most - in the hands of users. Unlike others in the space, who focus on the flashy problems, Valinor exists to solve the quiet, unaddressed problems-the small but significant ones that lead to cracks in the country's strength, stability, and security.

Backed by General Catalyst, Founders Fund, and Friends & Family Capital, Valinor was founded in 2024 and has established strategic partnerships with Palantir, Anduril, and Helsing. We are building the dream team, and we want you on it.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Valinor Harbor is seeking a Fabrication Lead to build and own the production capability behind its mobile, modular medical systems equipped for precision care, rapid response, and remote monitoring in austere environments. This is a hands-on leadership role for someone who can work directly with engineers to turn designs into manufacturable assemblies, run the shop floor, and manage the outside contractors who will execute a significant portion of the fabrication work. You'll own the project management side of that relationship, keeping contractors on schedule and on spec while maintaining quality standards. You'll work closely with engineers and fabrication staff to drive quality and efficiency from day one, and you'll set the standard for fabrication excellence across the team.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Translate engineering designs into fabrication plans, identifying opportunities to simplify construction and improve manufacturability

 - Source, manage, and coordinate outside fabrication contractors, serving as the primary project manager for contracted work from scoping through delivery

 - Fabricate and assemble modular structures and specialty enclosures using press brakes, laser cutting equipment, welding processes, and related tooling

 - Review drawings and collaborate directly with engineers to resolve design-for-fabrication issues before they reach the shop floor

 - Own shop quality and inspection, ensuring all assemblies, whether built in-house or by contractors, meet design requirements and production standards

 - Establish fabrication procedures, workflows, and documentation as the department's foundational systems

 - Continuously improve fabrication methods, tooling, and labor utilization to increase production efficiency

 - Build out the internal fabrication team over time, eventually supervising in-house fabricators and welders as the department grows

 - Maintain a safe, organized, and high-output shop environment




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 7+ years of hands-on fabrication experience, including structural steel and aluminum, sheet metal, and welding processes

 - Strong ability to read and interpret engineering drawings and fabrication packages with extreme attention to detail

 - Demonstrated experience in fabrication process planning and production workflow development

 - Experience leading or supervising a fabrication team, or clear readiness to step into that role

 - Proficiency with press brake and sheet metal fabrication equipment; CNC/laser programming experience a plus

 - Ability to edit CAD drawings a major plus

 - Background in military shelters or defense fabrication, oil and gas, or heavy equipment manufacturing strongly preferred

 - Experience with quality control and inspection processes for fabricated assemblies

 - AWS CWI certification or familiarity with D1.1 structural welding standards beneficial but not required




WHAT VALINOR OFFERS

 - Competitive salary, equity packages, and benefits, including health, dental, and vision insurance - fully covered for employees, 401(k), development stipends, among others.

 - Unlimited PTO and two-week company holiday at the end o

source_jobs.title: Mechanical Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Valinor is a new type of defense and government tech company focused on solving the problems others don't. Our unique model of centralized go-to-market and decentralized engineering allows us to move with unparalleled efficiency to identify unmet needs, build right-sized solutions, and get products where they matter most - in the hands of users. Unlike others in the space, who focus on the flashy problems, Valinor exists to solve the quiet, unaddressed problems-the small but significant ones that lead to cracks in the country's strength, stability, and security.

Backed by General Catalyst, Founders Fund, and Red Cell Partners, Valinor was founded in 2024 and has established strategic partnerships with Palantir, Anduril, and Helsing. We are building the dream team, and we want you on it.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Dispatch is developing ruggedized, field-deployable UxS charging infrastructure for defense applications and is seeking a Mechanical Engineer to support structural, thermal, and system-level design of next-generation platforms for deployment in austere environments.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Support the design, prototyping, and iteration of mechanical systems for autonomous charging infrastructure (e.g., docking stations), including enclosures, mounts, structural frames, and environmental protection systems, under the guidance of senior engineers.

 - Assist with structural, thermal, and dynamic analyses to help ensure reliability under military environmental conditions (MIL-STD-810, vibration, shock, EMI/EMC considerations).

 - Contribute to the development of mechanisms for automated docking, retention, alignment, and protection of payloads or vehicles.

 - Help evaluate and select materials and processes suitable for ruggedization, manufacturability, and weight/cost constraints.

 - Work closely with electrical and software teams to support integration of sensors, electronics, and electromechanical components.

 - Create and maintain CAD models, drawings, BOMs, and manufacturing documentation.

 - Support prototype fabrication, testing, and refinement in laboratory and field environments, including hands-on build and test tasks.




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - Bachelor's or Master's degree in Mechanical Engineering, Manufacturing/Industrial Engineering, Aerospace Engineering, or closely related field.

 - 1+ years of experience (including internships, co-ops, or strong project/coursework) in mechanical design, ideally for rugged, robotics, or mission-critical systems.

 - Proficiency with at least one CAD tool (SolidWorks, Fusion 360, or similar); exposure to mechanical analysis tools (FEA, CFD) is a plus.

 - Familiarity with, or strong interest in, designing for environmental and operational robustness - dust, water, salt, extreme temperatures, shock/vibration, and electromagnetic environments.

 - Exposure to AS9100/TDP/MIL-STD related items including MIL-STD-810, MIL-STD-461, or other military/DoD specifications, with willingness to learn (direct experience is a plus, not required).

 - Working knowledge of common manufacturing processes (machining, sheet metal, additive manufacturing, assembly), through coursework, projects, or work experience.

 - Hands-on aptitude and eagerness to learn rugged design practices, with mentorship provided.




WHAT VALINOR OFFERS

 - Competitive salary, equity packages, and benefits, including health, dental, and vision insurance - fully covered for employees, 401(k), among others.

 - Unlimited PTO and two-week company holiday at the end of every calendar year.

 - We are a pro-mental health and pro-family company - we actually encourage employees to spend time with themselves and their families. Valinor also provides fertility benefits to those just beginning that journey.

 - Fun work environment - we like to laugh and take care of each other, but we also deeply respect the mission in front of us.



Applicants must be a US Person and have the ability to obtain and maintain a U.S. se

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer, Edge AI
source_jobs.description:
Valinor is a new type of defense and government tech company focused on solving the problems others don't. Our unique model of centralized go-to-market and decentralized engineering allows us to move with unparalleled efficiency to identify unmet needs, build right-sized solutions, and get products where they matter most - in the hands of users. Unlike others in the space, who focus on the flashy problems, Valinor exists to solve the quiet, unaddressed problems-the small but significant ones that lead to cracks in the country's strength, stability, and security.

Backed by General Catalyst, Founders Fund, and Friends & Family Capital, Valinor was founded in 2024 and has established strategic partnerships with Palantir, Anduril, and Helsing. We are building the dream team, and we want you on it.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Valinor Reflex is seeking a Software Engineer to join our small, hands-on team building computer vision and wearable systems.

We're looking for a Software Engineer who can work across the full stack of an embedded system: from training a custom ML model, to writing firmware drivers, to building the interface the user actually touches. If you're energized by a fast-paced, build-first environment, we'd love to meet you.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

ML/AI Deployment

 - Deploy machine learning models on hardware-constrained devices

 - Evaluate current Open Source models and customize them to Reflex specific needs, optimizing performance through quantization and MLOps practices

 - Build and train custom models for customer use cases

 - Be able to articulate trade-offs and differences between different ML techniques

Hardware Integration & Embedded Systems

 - Write low-level code and interact directly with component drivers

 - Develop software to use wireless communication protocols (WiFi, Bluetooth, etc.)

 - Ensure robust communication and performance across embedded systems

Software Development

 - Build intuitive, modern GUI/UI across platforms

 - Manage user interaction with Reflex products ensuring a smooth UX

 - Develop software alongside hardware teams




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - Strong experience in embedded systems, microcontrollers, Linux development, and hardware constrained ML

 - Ability to prototype quickly and debug across hardware and software layers

 - Excited to build from the ground up in a startup environment

 - Willing to travel occasionally and collaborate hands-on with hardware in lab settings
   
   


WHAT VALINOR OFFERS

 - Competitive salary, equity packages, and benefits, including health, dental, and vision insurance - fully covered for employees, 401K, development stipends, among others.

 - Unlimited PTO and two-week company holiday at the end of every calendar year.

 - We are a pro-mental health and pro-family company - we actually encourage employees to spend time with themselves and their families. Valinor also provides fertility benefits to those just beginning that journey.

 - Fun work environment - we like to laugh and take care of each other, but we also deeply respect the mission in front of us.



This role requires the candidate be eligible to obtain and maintain a U.S. security clearance. Active clearance preferred.



Valinor is an equal-opportunity employer committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace. The Valinor team is made up of incredibly talented and unique individuals, who together are disrupting industry norms by creating new paths towards the future of defense and government technology. All qualified applicants will be treated with respect and receive equal consideration for employment without regard to race, color, creed, religion, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, uniform service, Veteran status, age, or any other protected characteristic per federal, state, or local law, including those with a criminal history, in a manner consistent with the requirements of applicable state and local laws, including the CA Fair

source_jobs.title: Construction Operations Technician
source_jobs.description:
Valinor is a new type of defense and government tech company focused on solving the problems others don’t. Our unique model of centralized go-to-market and decentralized engineering allows us to move with unparalleled efficiency to identify unmet needs, build right-sized solutions, and get products where they matter most - in the hands of users. Unlike others in the space, who focus on the flashy problems, Valinor exists to solve the quiet, unaddressed problems–the small but significant ones that lead to cracks in the country’s strength, stability, and security.

 

Backed by General Catalyst, Founders Fund, and Friends & Family Capital, Valinor was founded in 2024 and has established strategic partnerships with Palantir, Anduril, and Helsing. We are building the dream team, and we want you on it.

 


ABOUT THE ROLE

Valinor Harbor is seeking a Construction Operations Technician to support both field construction activities and day-to-day office operations for a growing technology company. This is a hands-on, flexible role for someone who is organized, resourceful, and comfortable shifting between shop/field work and administrative responsibilities. The ideal candidate is mechanically inclined, willing to learn, and capable of handling a wide variety of tasks — from basic construction work and project support to vendor coordination, sourcing materials, compliance assistance, and general office administration. This role offers opportunities to grow into crew coordination and day-to-day project management responsibilities.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

Construction & Field Support

 - Perform hands-on construction and top-out work including wall panels, painting, electrical assistance, installation, and general project support

 - Assist with framing, finishing, caulking, sealing, and hardware and fixture installation

 - Assist fabrication and production teams with project assembly

 - Maintain clean and organized job sites, shop areas, and tools

 - Support loading/unloading materials and inventory organization

 - Assist with field problem-solving and project punch list tasks

Operations & Administrative Support

 - Assist with operational and administrative tasks including sourcing materials, coordinating vendors, tracking orders, and supporting compliance documentation

 - Contact vendors and suppliers to negotiate pricing, availability, and delivery timelines

 - Help organize project documentation, drawings, and permit and inspection paperwork

 - Support scheduling and coordination of subcontractors and deliveries

 - Perform general office and administrative duties as needed

 - Assist leadership with miscellaneous operational tasks and project support

Cross-Functional

 - Help maintain organized workflows across shop, field, and office environments while supporting custom fabrication and modular construction projects

 - Grow into a role capable of coordinating small crews, overseeing finish quality, and helping manage day-to-day project execution
   
   


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - General construction experience preferred

 - Strong organizational and communication skills

 - Ability to multitask and adapt in a fast-paced environment

 - Comfortable working in both office and active shop/jobsite environments

 - Strong problem-solving mindset and willingness to learn

 - Basic computer proficiency including Outlook and Excel

 - Basic electrical, framing, wall paneling, or painting experience a plus

 - Experience sourcing materials or working with vendors/suppliers a plus

 - Forklift experience, OSHA or construction safety training is a plus

 - Valid driver's license preferred
   
   


WHAT VALINOR OFFERS

 - Competitive salary and benefits, including health, dental, and vision insurance — fully covered for employees, 401K, development stipends, among others.

 - Unlimited PTO and two-week company holiday at the end of every calendar year.

 - We are a pro-mental health and pro-family company — we actually encourage emp

source_jobs.title: Electrical Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Valinor is a new type of defense and government tech company focused on solving the problems others don't. Our unique model of centralized go-to-market and decentralized engineering allows us to move with unparalleled efficiency to identify unmet needs, build right-sized solutions, and get products where they matter most - in the hands of users. Unlike others in the space, who focus on the flashy problems, Valinor exists to solve the quiet, unaddressed problems-the small but significant ones that lead to cracks in the country's strength, stability, and security.

Backed by General Catalyst, Founders Fund, and Friends & Family Capital, Valinor was founded in 2024 and has established strategic partnerships with Palantir, Anduril, and Helsing. We are building the dream team, and we want you on it.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Valinor Reflex is seeking an Electrical Engineer to own key parts of the electronics behind its next-generation smart optics system, from circuit board design and PCB layout to hands-on assembly, sensor integration, board bring-up, and firmware/software for hardware control.

This is a hands-on role for someone who can work shoulder-to-shoulder with mechanical and software engineers on a small engineering team building AI-enabled wearables for mission-critical environments. You’ll help move the system from prototype to field-ready hardware and establish the electrical design standards the team will build on.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Design and develop circuit boards for Reflex hardware, including both schematic and layout

 - Build, assemble, test, and debug electronic devices, including glasses and peripheral hardware

 - Work hands-on with compact PCB assemblies, small components, stencils, and fine-pitch parts

 - Select parts and help shape system level design including power management, board-level architecture, and overall function of devices

 - Evaluate sensors, electronic components, and new hardware integrations for future product capabilities

 - Write firmware and software for hardware control, testing, debugging, and device operation

 - Support board bring-up, failure analysis, and fast iteration across prototype builds

 - Collaborate closely with the mechanical engineering team to integrate electronics into wearable form factors

 - Help move hardware quickly from early prototype to functional, field-ready systems




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - Bachelor’s degree in Electrical Engineering, Computer Engineering, or a closely related field

 - 2–4 years of electrical engineering experience, ideally with hands-on hardware development

 - Experience building electronic devices, circuit boards, or hardware prototypes

 - Strong experience designing PCBs, including schematic capture and layout

 - Comfort assembling, soldering, testing, and debugging boards by hand

 - Experience working with small components, ideally down to 0402-level parts, stencils, and small-pitch components

 - Experience with firmware or software for hardware control, testing, or embedded systems

 - Ability to select components, evaluate electrical tradeoffs, and contribute to power management design

 - Comfort using bench equipment, debugging hardware issues, and working through prototype ambiguity

 - Experience working closely with mechanical engineers or integrating electronics into physical products




WHAT VALINOR OFFERS

 - Competitive salary, equity packages, and benefits, including health, dental, and vision insurance - fully covered for employees, 401K, development stipends, among others.

 - Unlimited PTO and two-week company holiday at the end of every calendar year.

 - We are a pro-mental health and pro-family company - we actually encourage employees to spend time with themselves and their families. Valinor also provides fertility benefits to those just beginning that journey.

 - Fun work environment - we like to laugh and take care of each other, but we also deeply respect the mission in front of us.

source_jobs.title: General Application
source_jobs.description:
Don’t see a role that fits your background?



We’re tackling hard, meaningful problems and believe great people don’t always fit neatly into predefined roles. If you’re excited about our mission but don’t see an open role that’s a clear match today, you can apply here to share your background and the kinds of problems you’re most excited to work on.



We review these applications regularly and will reach out if there’s a potential fit, either now or as new roles open.



Valinor is a new type of defense and government tech company focused on solving the problems others don’t. Our unique model of centralized go-to-market and decentralized engineering allows us to move with unparalleled efficiency to identify unmet needs, build right-sized solutions, and get products where they matter most - in the hands of users. Unlike others in the space, who focus on the flashy problems, Valinor exists to solve the quiet, unaddressed problems–the small but significant ones that lead to cracks in the country’s strength, stability, and security.



Backed by General Catalyst, Founders Fund, and Friends & Family Capital, Valinor was founded in 2024 and has established strategic partnerships with Palantir, Anduril, and Helsing. We are building the dream team, and we want you on it.



Valinor is an equal-opportunity employer committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace. The Valinor team is made up of incredibly talented and unique individuals, who together are disrupting industry norms by creating new paths towards the future of defense and government technology. All qualified applicants will be treated with respect and receive equal consideration for employment without regard to race, color, creed, religion, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, uniform service, Veteran status, age, or any other protected characteristic per federal, state, or local law, including those with a criminal history, in a manner consistent with the requirements of applicable state and local laws, including the CA Fair Chance Initiative for Hiring Ordinance. We actively encourage members of recognized minorities, women, Veterans, and those with disabilities to apply, and we work to create a welcoming and supportive environment for all applicants throughout the interview process. If you are someone passionate about working on problems that have a real-world impact, we'd love to hear from you!

source_jobs.title: Lead Mechanical Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Valinor is a new type of defense and government tech company focused on solving the problems others don't. Our unique model of centralized go-to-market and decentralized engineering allows us to move with unparalleled efficiency to identify unmet needs, build right-sized solutions, and get products where they matter most - in the hands of users. Unlike others in the space, who focus on the flashy problems, Valinor exists to solve the quiet, unaddressed problems-the small but significant ones that lead to cracks in the country's strength, stability, and security.

Backed by General Catalyst, Founders Fund, and Friends & Family Capital, Valinor was founded in 2024 and has established strategic partnerships with Palantir, Anduril, and Helsing. We are building the dream team, and we want you on it.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Valinor Harbor is seeking a Lead Mechanical Engineer to own the design and engineer its mobile, modular medical systems equipped for precision care, rapid response, and remote monitoring in austere environments. This is a hands-on technical leadership role for someone who is equally comfortable with a CAD model and a fabrication shop floor. You'll work closely with fabrication teams, project managers, and customers to take concepts from initial design through production-ready documentation, and you'll set the standard for engineering quality across the team.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Design and engineer structural modifications for CONEX/shipping containers for defense, commercial, industrial, and specialty applications

 - Create detailed 2D and 3D CAD models, assemblies, and fabrication drawings using industry-standard CAD software

 - Develop construction and fabrication plans suitable for manufacturing and field installation

 - Perform structural analysis and simulation testing to validate design integrity, load capacity, and code compliance

 - Collaborate with fabrication teams, project managers, and clients to ensure designs meet project requirements and production capabilities

 - Review and optimize designs for manufacturability, cost efficiency, and structural performance

 - Produce BOMs (Bills of Materials), technical documentation, and engineering change revisions

 - Ensure all designs comply with applicable engineering standards, safety regulations, and local building codes

 - Support prototype development, testing, and implementation of container modification projects

 - Conduct site visits and fabrication shop reviews as needed to verify measurements and construction accuracy
   
   


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - Bachelor's degree in Mechanical Engineering, Structural Engineering, Design Engineering, or a related field preferred

 - Proficiency with CAD software such as SolidWorks, AutoCAD, Inventor, Fusion 360, or similar platforms

 - Prior experience designing modifications to shipping containers, steel structures, modular buildings, or fabricated assemblies strongly preferred

 - Background in heavy equipment, trailers, modular buildings, defense, marine, oilfield, or industrial equipment preferred

 - Strong understanding of welding, fabrication processes, and structural steel design — hands-on shop experience a plus

 - Experience creating production-ready fabrication drawings, shop packages, and supporting documentation for certification

 - Experience with structural load paths, dynamic loading, transportable structures, and corrosion/coating considerations including mixed material interfaces

 - Experience supporting prototype-to-production transitions and managing multiple projects simultaneously

 - FEA/simulation analysis experience preferred; PE or EIT beneficial but not required

 - Familiarity with relevant standards a plus: ISO/CSC, ABS, DNV, ASME/AWS, UFC, or military specifications
   
   


WHAT VALINOR OFFERS

 - Competitive salary, equity packages, and benefits, including health, dental, and vision insurance - fully covered for employees, 401K, development stipends, among others.

source_jobs.title: Full-Stack Software Engineer, C2 & Tactical Integration
source_jobs.description:
Valinor is a new type of defense and government tech company focused on solving the problems others don't. Our unique model of centralized go-to-market and decentralized engineering allows us to move with unparalleled efficiency to identify unmet needs, build right-sized solutions, and get products where they matter most - in the hands of users. Unlike others in the space, who focus on the flashy problems, Valinor exists to solve the quiet, unaddressed problems-the small but significant ones that lead to cracks in the country's strength, stability, and security.

Backed by General Catalyst, Founders Fund, and Red Cell Partners, Valinor was founded in 2024 and has established strategic partnerships with Palantir, Anduril, and Helsing. We are building the dream team, and we want you on it.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Dispatch is developing ruggedized, field-deployable UxS charging infrastructure for defense applications and is seeking a Software Engineer to support the development of related software for next-generation autonomous systems support platforms for deployment in austere environments.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Implement and maintain features across the full stack for command and control (C2) applications, including web-based operator interfaces, backend services, and data integration layers, under the guidance of senior engineers.

 - Build responsive front-end components for real-time situational awareness, mission planning, and asset management using modern frameworks.

 - Develop and maintain backend services, APIs, and data pipelines that support telemetry ingestion and mission-critical workflows.

 - Assist in integrating C2 software with tactical systems and platforms such as Lattice, ATAK, and other interoperability standards (CoT, TAK protocols, etc.).

 - Help debug and troubleshoot networking and data-flow issues across the stack, applying a working understanding of the OSI model, particularly the lower/base layers (physical, data link, network, transport), to trace connectivity, latency, and packet-level problems.

 - Collaborate with autonomy, robotics, and systems engineering teams to surface vehicle/asset status, sensor feeds, and control interfaces within operator dashboards.

 - Support verification, validation, and testing activities, including simulation environments, integration testing, and operational exercises.

 - Contribute to documentation including API specifications and interface control documents (ICDs) in compliance with federal defense standards.




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or related field (or equivalent practical experience).

 - 1+ years of experience (including internships, co-ops, or strong project work) in full-stack software development with exposure to modern front-end frameworks and backend technologies.

 - Working knowledge of the OSI model, particularly the lower/base layers (physical, data link, network, transport), and basic networking fundamentals (TCP/IP, UDP, sockets, routing).

 - Exposure to real-time data visualization, mapping/GIS technologies, or WebSocket / pub-sub architectures (through coursework, projects, or work experience).

 - Familiarity with, or strong interest in, tactical data standards and military C2 ecosystems (ATAK, TAK Server, Cursor on Target, Lattice, or similar) - direct experience is a plus, not required.

 - Basic understanding of containerization and microservices (Docker, Kubernetes) and cloud services (AWS/Azure).

 - Exposure to version control (Git) and CI/CD pipelines for building and testing software.

 - Eagerness to learn defense software development processes, secure coding practices, and system hardening, with mentorship provided.




WHAT VALINOR OFFERS

 - Competitive salary, equity packages, and benefits, including health, dental, and vision insurance - fully covered for employees, 401(k), among others.

 - Unlimited PTO and two-week company holiday at the end of ever

source_jobs.title: Lead Robotics Engineer, Autonomy
source_jobs.description:
Valinor is a new type of defense and government tech company focused on solving the problems others don't. Our unique model of centralized go-to-market and decentralized engineering allows us to move with unparalleled efficiency to identify unmet needs, build right-sized solutions, and get products where they matter most - in the hands of users. Unlike others in the space, who focus on the flashy problems, Valinor exists to solve the quiet, unaddressed problems-the small but significant ones that lead to cracks in the country's strength, stability, and security.

Backed by General Catalyst, Founders Fund, and Red Cell Partners, Valinor was founded in 2024 and has established strategic partnerships with Palantir, Anduril, and Helsing. We are building the dream team, and we want you on it.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Valinor Dispatch is seeking a Lead Robotics Engineer to own the autonomy stack end to end, from computer vision and flight control to robotic manipulation, behind its ruggedized, field-deployable UxS charging infrastructure for defense applications. This is a hands-on technical leadership role for someone who can set architectural direction, work shoulder-to-shoulder with mechanical, electrical, and software teams, and mentor a small group of engineers building the next generation of autonomous and robotic platforms. You'll drive the system from concept through field deployment and establish the technical standards the team will build on.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Architect and own the full autonomy stack (perception, state estimation, planning, and control) across autonomous aerial platforms and robotic manipulators, from concept through field deployment.

 - Lead integration and tuning of flight control software (PX4/ArduPilot-class or in-house controllers), including attitude and position loops for stability and reliable performance in real-world and contested environments.

 - Design, develop, and bring up robotic arm systems including kinematics, dynamics, motion planning, and manipulation behaviors, with sim-to-real validation and on-hardware tuning.

 - Develop computer vision and sensor-fusion pipelines (camera/IMU/LiDAR) for navigation, target detection, alignment, and autonomous docking and retention tasks.

 - Work closely with mechanical, electrical, and software teams to ensure seamless integration of sensors, electronics, and electromechanical components into deployable systems.

 - Establish standards for simulation, testing, and safe field operation. Drive technical direction through design reviews and architecture decisions.

 - Mentor a small team of robotics and software engineers. Lead prototype bring-up, testing, and refinement in lab and field environments.




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - Bachelor's or Master's in Robotics, EE/ME, Aerospace Engineering, Computer Engineering, or a closely related field.

 - 5+ years of experience building robotics/autonomy systems, with 2+ years in a technical lead or team-lead capacity on mission-critical or fielded systems (defense, aerospace, drones, or similar).

 - Strong proficiency in C++ and Python, with solid real-time and embedded systems fundamentals.

 - Demonstrated depth across the full stack: perception/CV, state estimation (EKF/factor graphs, VIO/SLAM), motion and path planning, and control (PID through MPC).

 - Hands-on experience with flight control stacks (PX4/ArduPilot or equivalent) and tuning controllers on real hardware.

 - Experience designing and deploying 6DOF manipulation systems, including kinematics, motion planning (MoveIt/OMPL-class), and grasping.

 - Proficiency with ROS/ROS2 and robotics simulation tooling (Gazebo, Isaac Sim, or similar), plus hardware-in-the-loop testing.

 - Track record of shipping autonomous systems to the real world, not just simulation or research, with sound judgment on sim-to-real and field reliability.

 - Robot learning (imitation/RL, VLA models), CUDA/edge deployment, and experience operating in rugged or conte

notes: Defense and government tech company with a strong focus on engineering roles
